examples: He accused the mainstream political parties of cynically exploiting this situation.
Popularly, one refers cynically to "human nature" in accepting the inevitability of such undesirable human behavior as greed, murder, cheating and lying.
In private this was sometimes cynically admitted.
We somewhat cynically predicted his proposal would be rejected by his fellow board members.
One should regard cynically any argument that depends on the stereotype of woman as helpless victim.
